basic_profile.legal_system: Civil law (codified Civil Code + Family Code); notarial/registry-based succession
basic_profile.currency: Fully dollarized (US dollar legal tender since 2001)

capital_controls.value: Dollarized economy; no domestic monetary policy

general_attitude_bitcoin.value: Bitcoin is legal but acceptance is VOLUNTARY as of the 29 Jan 2025 amendment; it is NO LONGER legal tender and taxes can no longer be paid in BTC. The state retains a strategic Bitcoin reserve

tax_residence_basis.value: Territorial system; foreign-source income generally not taxed for residents. Exact day-count and tie-breakers not confirmed against a Tier-1 source

crypto_specific_guidance.value: Secondary sources report a Bitcoin capital-gains exemption survived the Jan 2025 amendments (the ability to PAY taxes in BTC was repealed, not the gains exemption), but this is NOT confirmed against a Tier-1 Ministerio de Hacienda source

legal_to_own.value: LEAD (Digital Assets Issuance Law, 2023) regulates issuers/providers; the CNAD (Comisión Nacional de Activos Digitales) licenses and supervises

self_custody_allowed.value: Individual holding/self-custody and P2P use remain legal post-2025; the amendment removed mandatory acceptance, not the right to hold. No Tier-1 statute text on self-custody located

direction.value: Under the IMF EFF (approved 26 Feb 2025) public-sector Bitcoin activity is limited and the state Chivo wallet is being divested; private digital-asset business incentives (LEAD) were not reported repealed

carf_status.value: El Salvador was reported among non-committed CARF 'jurisdictions of relevance' with weak/absent CRS participation, making it a present-state transparency outlier. Note: the CARF committed list has grown (60+ jurisdictions); re-confirm El Salvador's current status against the live OECD CARF commitments list
carf_status.note: Downgraded Medium->Low in the 2026-06-16 verification pass after the Panama CARF conflict showed the committed list is moving; re-verify.

forced_heirship.value: Bello-derived Civil Code. INTESTATE succession calls children, parents, and spouse by order, dividing equally within each order (confirmed). Whether a testate forced-heirship reserve (legítima) applies, and its fraction, was not confirmed against the current Code this pass and should be treated as an open question
forced_heirship.note: Intestate equal-share confirmed 2026-06-16 against the Código Civil; testate legítima fraction NOT confirmed - do not assert a forced-heirship reserve without checking the Code.

dollar_access.value: Mature USD banking sector; a dedicated banking framework aimed at digital-asset/sophisticated investors has been promoted; live status should be confirmed against BCR/Superintendencia

citizenship_path.value: A citizenship-by-investment 'Freedom Visa' (launched Dec 2024) is reported to require a ~US$1,000,000 BTC/USDT contribution; terms and caps must be confirmed with the issuing authority. A 90-day residency-presence reform is reported but the decree/effective date needs primary confirmation

payments.value: Merchants are no longer required to accept Bitcoin (voluntary since Jan 2025); holding and P2P use remain legal

direction_of_travel.label: self-custody-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: Self-custody and P2P use remain legal; LEAD provides a digital-asset business regime; low present-state automatic reporting
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Legal-tender mandate repealed; tax-exemption persistence post-IMF is reported only by secondary sources; IMF conditionality could tighten

direction_of_travel.label: unclear-or-changing
direction_of_travel.rationale: Bitcoin policy shifted materially under the IMF program in 2025; several incentives' current status is unconfirmed
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Core LEAD framework and state reserve persist

open_questions.question: Confirm against a Tier-1 Ministerio de Hacienda source whether the Bitcoin capital-gains exemption survived the Jan 2025 amendments and the IMF program
Post-IMF status of LEAD 0% digital-asset incentives
Exact tax-residence day-count and whether territorial regime applies by source or requires formal residency
Freedom Visa current terms, accepted assets, cap and AML screening; the 90-day residency-presence decree number and effective date
Chivo divestiture outcome and disposition of user balances

watchlist.item: Bitcoin legal-tender repeal (29 Jan 2025) and IMF EFF conditions
Volcano Bond issuance status (confirm against CNAD/issuer prospectus)
Residency-presence reform (reported 90-day rule) pending decree confirmation

product_routing_notes: El Salvador is the clearest stale-claim test in the cohort. Users often arrive with outdated 'legal tender' assumptions; the audit should correct to the voluntary-acceptance reality and route mobility/tax questions to professionals.

change_log.change: Initial profile from parallel research pass. CRITICAL: corrected the legal-tender status to voluntary acceptance (repealed 29 Jan 2025) sourced to IMF program docs + reform reporting. Tax incentives flagged Low/Unknown pending Tier-1 Hacienda confirmation.
